mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during periods of elevated inflation indicates capacity constraints that force monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 210 and INDPRO rises, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL is below 200.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes remains below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 215 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling unemployment confirms sustainable economic expansion, eliminating recession fears and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year window, or 60-day returns are negative during confirmed expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signal demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when INDPRO falls and FEDFUNDS rises, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS is stable or declining.
